title: Gymnorebutia buiningiana
taxonomy: Gymnocactus buiningiana, also known as Gymnorebutia buiningiana, is a species of flowering plant in the cactus family, Cactaceae. It is native to Bolivia and is a member of the genus Gymnocactus, although some sources place it in the genus Rebutia. The plant is known for its small to medium size and its globular to cylindrical stems. Gymnocactus buiningiana has short, fine spines and produces funnel-shaped flowers in shades of yellow and white. The classification of this species has undergone changes over time, reflecting ongoing research and understanding of its relationships within the cactus family.

intro: Gymnorebutia buiningiana, commonly known as the 'Dwarf Rebutia', is a species of cactus in the genus Gymnorebutia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may aid in the treatment of various conditions, such as arthritis and skin disorders. The bioactive constituents of Gymnorebutia buiningiana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Gymnorebutia buiningiana is often used to prepare tinctures, capsules, and topical creams, which are used to treat various health issues, including wounds, infections, and skin conditions.
